zh.lifeSummary: 當Maria van Brabant於1254年5月13日出生在比利時布拉班特洛文時，她的父親Hendrik III 'de Zachtmoedige' van Brabant24歲，母親Adélaïde de Bourgogne21歲。她於1274年8月21日與Philippe III le Hardi roi de France在法國i萊-德-福朗斯瓦爾-德-馬內文斯結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1322年1月12日在法國瑟-吉瓦萊斯 姆斯奧享年67歲，埋葬在法國i萊-德-福朗斯芭黎絲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Maria van Brabant于1254年5月13日出生在比利时布拉班特洛文时，她的父亲Hendrik III 'de Zachtmoedige' van Brabant24岁，母亲Adélaïde de Bourgogne21岁。她于1274年8月21日与Philippe III le Hardi roi de France在法国i萊-德-福朗斯瓦爾-德-馬內文斯结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1322年1月12日在法国瑟-吉瓦萊斯 姆斯奧享年67岁，埋葬在法国i萊-德-福朗斯芭黎絲。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Brabant
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Aime, Aristide, Marcel, Normand, Ovila, Yvan.
French, Walloon, Flemish, and German: habitational name from Brabant, a province in the Low Countries.

English: cognate of 1 above, from Old French Brabant, Middle English Braban, Brabayn, used to denote both the region and an inhabitant of it. See also the synonymous Brabazon . In medieval England, Brabans were typically linen weavers and cloth merchants. The final syllable of Braban, when weakly stressed, was often altered to -ben, -bin, or -bon, and also to -bam. The additional -t or -d in post-medieval Brabant, Braband , etc. is probably excrescent, rather than a relic of the Old French form.

variants.name: Brabender
meanings: German (mainly Westphalia): habitational name for someone from Brabant (see   Brabant  ), perhaps applied also as a nickname for a merchant of Brabantine cloth.

variants.name: Brabazon
meanings: English: from Old French Brabançon, Anglo-Norman French Brabanzon, Middle English Brab(a)son ‘Brabanter’. This was originally an ethnic name for a native of the duchy of Brabant (now in north Belgium). Medieval Brabant was widely known for two exports, its brutal mercenary soldiers and its linen cloth. In the 13th century parts of France were devastated by marauding gangs of armed Brabanters, and brabançon came to be used generically to denote a mercenary, hired assassin, or robber. These originated in Brabant and Flanders, but in the course of time accepted recruits from anywhere. The true source of the surname is the presence in many English (and Irish) towns of Brabant cloth merchants.

variants.name: Bremner
meanings: Scottish: habitational name for someone from Brabant in the Low Countries, from Older Scots Brebner, Brabanare ‘native or inhabitant of Brabant’ (see Brabant and compare with the synonymous Brabazon ). Weavers from Brabant settled early at Aberdeen and on the east coast of Scotland.
German: habitational name for someone from the city of Bremen. This surname is very rare in Germany.

variants.name: Brabham
meanings: English: variant of Brabant , altered by association with English habitational names ending in -ham.

variants.name: Braband
meanings: German and English: variant of   Brabant  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Maria
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Maria  , of German origin but now also moderately popular in the English-speaking world.

en.lifeSummary: When Maria van Brabant was born on 13 May 1254, in Leuven, Brabant, Belgium, her father, Hendrik III 'de Zachtmoedige' van Brabant, was 24 and her mother, Adélaïde de Bourgogne, was 21. She married Philippe III le Hardi roi de France on 21 August 1274, in Vincennes, Val-de-Marne, Île-de-France, France. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died on 12 January 1322, in Les Mureaux, Seine-et-Oise, France, at the age of 67, and was buried in Paris, Île-de-France, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Maria van Brabant est née le 13 mai 1254, à Leuven, Brabant, Belgique, son père, Hendrik III 'de Zachtmoedige' van Brabant, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Adélaïde de Bourgogne, avait 21 ans. Elle a épousé Philippe III le Hardi roi de France le 21 août 1274, à Vincennes, Val-de-Marne, Île-de-France, France. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 12 janvier 1322, à Les Mureaux, Seine-et-Oise, France, à l’âge de 67 ans, et a été enterrée à Paris, Île-de-France, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Maria van Brabant am 13. Mai 1254 in Leuven, Brabant, Belgien geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Hendrik III 'de Zachtmoedige' van Brabant 24 und ihre Mutter Adélaïde de Bourgogne 21. Sie hat am 21. August 1274 in Vincennes, Val-de-Marne, Île-de-France, Frankreich Philippe III le Hardi roi de France geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 12. Januar 1322 im Alter von 67 Jahren in Les Mureaux, Seine-et-Oise, Frankreich und wurde in Paris, Île-de-France, Frankreich begraben.

Lei sposato Philippe III le Hardi roi de France il 21 agosto 1274, nel Vincennes, Val-de-Marne, Île-de-France, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì il 12 gennaio 1322, nel Les Mureaux, Seine-et-Oise, Francia, all'età di 67 anni, e fu sepolta a Paris, Île-de-France, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Maria van Brabantが1254年5月13日にベルギーブラバントレウベンで生まれた時，父Hendrik III 'de Zachtmoedige' van Brabantは24歳，母Adélaïde de Bourgogneは21歳であった。1274年8月21日にフランスiレ-デ-フランスヴァル-デ-マルンヴィンセンスでPhilippe III le Hardi roi de Franceと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1322年1月12日にフランスセアイネ-エト-オイゼレス ムレアウックスで67歳で死去し，フランスiレ-デ-フランスパーイスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Maria van Brabant(이)가 1254년 5월 13일 벨기에 브라반트 레우븐에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Hendrik III 'de Zachtmoedige' van Brabant(은)는 24세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Adélaïde de Bourgogne(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 1274년 8월 21일 프랑스 i레-데-프랜스 발-데-마니 빈스네스에서 Philippe III le Hardi roi de France(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1322년 1월 12일 프랑스 세이인-트-이스 레스 머이스에서 67세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 i레-데-프랜스 파리스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Maria van Brabant nasceu em 13 de maio de 1254, em Leuven, Brabante, Bélgica, seu pai, Hendrik III 'de Zachtmoedige' van Brabant, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Adélaïde de Bourgogne, tinha 21 anos. Ela casou-se com Philippe III le Hardi roi de France em 21 de agosto de 1274, em Vincennes, Val-de-Marne, Île-de-France, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 12 de janeiro de 1322, em Les Mureaux, Seine-et-Oise, França, com 67 anos, e foi sepultada em Paris, Île-de-France, França.

ru.Marlborough Castle, Marlborough, Wiltshi: марлбороую ст питир анд ст поул, уилцхир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Maria van Brabant родилась 13 мая 1254 г. в местности леувен, брабант, Бельгия, её отцу по имени Hendrik III 'de Zachtmoedige' van Brabant было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Adélaïde de Bourgogne, 21. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Philippe III le Hardi roi de France 21 августа 1274 г. в местности винценнесь, вал-де-марн, iле-де-фрэнс, Франция. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Она умерла 12 января 1322 г. в местности лесь мурокс, сине-ет-оис, Франция в возрасте 67 лет и была похоронена в местности пэрис, iле-де-фрэнс, Франция.

es.Marlborough Castle, Marlborough, Wiltshi: Marlborough St Peter and St Paul, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Maria van Brabant nació el 13 de mayo de 1254, en Leuven, Brabante, Bélgica, su papá, Hendrik III 'de Zachtmoedige' van Brabant, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Adélaïde de Bourgogne, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Philippe III le Hardi roi de France el 21 de agosto de 1274, en Vincennes, Val-de-Marne, Île-de-France, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió el 12 de enero de 1322, en Les Mureaux, Seine-et-Oise, Francia, a la edad de 67 años, y fue sepultada en Paris, Île-de-France, Francia.
